personal_projects.title: AI-Powered Interactive Resume (this site)
personal_projects.description: Designed and built this site as a full-stack demonstration of AI integration into a practical system. The core is a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: structured data about my background is embedded using OpenAI's embedding models, and user questions are matched to the most semantically relevant context before being passed to an LLM. The result is a conversational AI that answers questions accurately from a custom knowledge base rather than hallucinating. Built on Node.js, deployed on Vercel. This project reflects the exact kind of work I want to do professionally — taking an existing communication problem (a static resume) and solving it by integrating AI into the workflow.

personal_projects.title: News Aggregation & User Platform
personal_projects.description: Built a full-stack platform that pulls from multiple global news sources, categorizes articles into topic threads, and lets users explore without single-outlet bias. Added user accounts, real-time WebSocket chat, and a dashboard. I owned everything: database schema, authentication architecture, background aggregator, real-time layer, and frontend. The security architecture — password hashing, session token design, route protection — required the most research and produced the most durable learning. This was my first experience designing a system end-to-end and understanding how each layer depends on the others.

personal_projects.title: AI Workflow Automation Experiments
personal_projects.description: Ongoing series of experiments integrating AI tooling into real workflows — automating repetitive tasks, building LLM-assisted scripts, and evaluating where AI genuinely reduces friction versus where it adds complexity. Focus is on practical implementation: what does it actually take to get an AI tool working reliably inside an existing process? Projects include prompt engineering for structured output, AI-assisted documentation generation, and lightweight agentic task runners.

repositories.name: LIS 4376 - AI Applications
repositories.description: Coursework for Applied AI — covering ML model evaluation, NLP, AI ethics, and real-world deployment considerations. Emphasis on critically evaluating AI outputs, not just implementing them. Developed ability to assess where AI creates genuine value versus where it introduces risk or unnecessary complexity.

repositories.name: LIS 4381 - Mobile Web Development
repositories.description: Responsive, mobile-first web applications with focus on system architecture, CSS layout systems, and cross-device performance. Reinforced the discipline of stripping solutions to their minimum effective form — a principle that applies directly to systems consulting.

project_interests: AI integration into existing enterprise systems
RAG pipeline design for organizational knowledge bases
Workflow automation using LLMs and scripting
Systems architecture for scalable and maintainable IT infrastructure
Consulting tools that translate system data into business insight
Agentic AI workflows for process automation
